name: Mary Geoise
description: Mary Geoise is the capital of the World Government, located at the summit of the Red Line directly above Fish-Man Island. It serves as the residence of the World Nobles (Celestial Dragons) and the seat of absolute power in the One Piece world. The city is a symbol of divine authority and untouchable privilege, where the descendants of the twenty founding kingdoms live in opulent luxury while the rest of the world suffers. It houses Pangaea Castle, where the Five Elders conduct the world's governance and where the mysterious Im sits on the Empty Throne. Every four years, the Reverie (World Summit) is held here, bringing together the leaders of all World Government-affiliated nations. The city represents the pinnacle of inequality, built on the backs of slavery, exploitation, and the erasure of history. Its very existence atop the Red Line divides the world's oceans and symbolizes the separation between the 'gods' and common people.
history: Mary Geoise was established 800 years ago after the Void Century when twenty royal families formed the World Government and defeated the Ancient Kingdom. These families became the World Nobles (Celestial Dragons), moving to Mary Geoise and ruling from above the world. The Nefertari Family of Alabasta famously refused to ascend, remaining with their people. The Empty Throne was created as a symbol that no single king rules the world, though the existence of Im secretly sitting on it reveals this to be a lie. For centuries, Mary Geoise has been the untouchable center of power, protected by Marine Admirals and CP0. The city became a focal point during Fisher Tiger's historic raid 15 years before the main story, when he climbed the Red Line with his bare hands and freed thousands of slaves, setting fire to the city. During the recent Reverie, major events shook Mary Geoise: the Revolutionary Army commanders infiltrated to rescue Bartholomew Kuma, Sabo witnessed Im and the Five Elders' true nature, the murder of Cobra occurred, and Im destroyed Lulusia Kingdom from Mary Geoise using an ancient weapon. These events revealed the dark truth behind the World Government's authority and sparked global upheaval.
culture: The culture of Mary Geoise is defined by absolute supremacy and divine privilege. The Celestial Dragons believe themselves to be gods descended from the creators of the world, viewing all others as beneath them. They wear bubble helmets to avoid breathing the same air as commoners and ride on the backs of slaves. Human slavery is legal and normalized here, with slaves bearing the 'Hoof of the Soaring Dragon' brand. The city operates under different laws than the rest of the world - Celestial Dragons can kill, enslave, or abuse anyone without consequence, and any who strike a World Noble face execution by Marine Admiral. Culture includes lavish parties, human auctions, and displays of excessive wealth. The architecture is pristine and grand, reflecting ancient Roman and Greek aesthetics mixed with futuristic elements. The Reverie is the only time outsiders are permitted, bringing world leaders to discuss global issues - though the Five Elders make all real decisions. Despite the appearance of democracy, Mary Geoise is a totalitarian regime where Im's will is absolute. The culture prioritizes the erasure of history, the suppression of D. clan knowledge, and the maintenance of the status quo at any cost.

industries: Heavenly Tribute collection (taxation of member nations)
economy.currency: Berry (฿) - collected as tribute from all member nations

transportation: Access to Mary Geoise is highly restricted. The primary route is via the Bond Bridge elevator system that connects the base of the Red Line to the summit. Visitors must have permission and undergo strict security. Celestial Dragons travel via slave-carried palanquins or riding on the backs of enslaved humans. The Travelators (moving walkways) connect different districts of the city. For departures, there are designated ports and the Bond Bridge descent. The Five Elders and Im likely have access to secret passages and teleportation methods. Fisher Tiger famously climbed the Red Line with his bare hands, an 'impossible' feat that shocked the world. The city's position atop the Red Line makes it nearly impregnable, with only the front gate accessible - any other approach requires scaling a 10,000-meter cliff.

cuisine: Rare meats and seafood from around the world
Gourmet dishes prepared by world-class chefs (often slaves)
traditions: Celestial Dragons eating while slaves starve
Food fights and waste as entertainment
Competitive displays of wealth through food
quirks: Food is treated carelessly despite its value
Celestial Dragons demand rare and exotic items
Wasting food is common and expected
Some dishes cost more than a commoner's lifetime earnings

notablePeople.name: Im
notablePeople.role: Secret Sovereign of the World / The One Who Sits on the Empty Throne
notablePeople.description: The mysterious supreme leader of the world who secretly rules from the Empty Throne that is supposed to remain vacant. Possesses the power to erase entire islands and has lived for centuries. Only the Five Elders know of Im's existence. Holds a particular interest in those bearing the Will of D.

notablePeople.name: Saint Jaygarcia Saturn
notablePeople.role: Five Elders - Warrior God of Science and Defense
notablePeople.description: One of the Five Elders who personally traveled to Egghead. Possesses a monstrous yokai transformation and near-immortality. Commands the World Government's scientific and defensive operations.

notablePeople.name: Saint Marcus Mars
notablePeople.role: Five Elders - Warrior God of Environment
notablePeople.description: One of the Five Elders with authority over environmental matters. Possesses a bird-like yokai transformation with immense power.

notablePeople.name: Saint Topman Warcury
notablePeople.role: Five Elders - Warrior God of Justice
notablePeople.description: One of the Five Elders overseeing justice and law. Transforms into a massive boar-like yokai with devastating power.

notablePeople.name: Saint Ethanbaron V. Nusjuro
notablePeople.role: Five Elders - Warrior God of Finance
notablePeople.description: One of the Five Elders controlling financial matters. A master swordsman who transforms into a skeletal horse yokai.

notablePeople.name: Saint Shepherd Ju Peter
notablePeople.role: Five Elders - Warrior God of Agriculture
notablePeople.description: One of the Five Elders overseeing agriculture and land. Transforms into a giant sandworm-like yokai.

notablePeople.name: Saint Donquixote Mjosgard
notablePeople.description: The only known Celestial Dragon to abandon his supremacist beliefs after being saved by Queen Otohime. Executed for defending Shirahoshi and opposing his fellow World Nobles during the Reverie.

notablePeople.name: Saint Charlos
notablePeople.description: An arrogant and cruel World Noble who attempted to enslave Shirahoshi during the Reverie. Represents the worst of Celestial Dragon culture. Son of Saint Rosward.

notablePeople.name: Saint Rosward
notablePeople.description: Father of Charlos and Shalria. A typical World Noble who treats others as property and objects. Epitomizes Celestial Dragon entitlement.

notablePeople.name: Sabo
notablePeople.role: Revolutionary Army Chief of Staff / Flame Emperor
notablePeople.description: Luffy and Ace's sworn brother who infiltrated Mary Geoise during the Reverie. Witnessed Im's existence and Cobra's murder. Became known as the 'Flame Emperor' and was falsely accused of assassinating King Cobra.

notablePeople.name: Nefertari Cobra
notablePeople.description: The king who questioned the Five Elders about the Void Century and the Nefertari Family's connection to the Poneglyphs. Murdered by Im for possessing forbidden knowledge, but passed crucial information to Sabo before dying.

notablePeople.name: Nefertari Vivi
notablePeople.description: Princess of Alabasta and honorary Straw Hat member who attended the Reverie. Went missing after her father's assassination, with Wapol claiming to have witnessed something significant involving her.

notablePeople.name: Bartholomew Kuma
notablePeople.description: The former Revolutionary turned into a mindless slave for the Celestial Dragons after his deal with the World Government. Was rented out to Celestial Dragons as a plaything before being rescued during the Reverie.

notablePeople.name: Monkey D. Garp
notablePeople.description: The legendary Marine who refused promotion to Admiral to avoid serving the Celestial Dragons directly. Represents the conflict between justice and the corrupt system of Mary Geoise.

notablePeople.name: Shirahoshi
notablePeople.role: Princess of Ryugu Kingdom / Ancient Weapon Poseidon
notablePeople.description: The mermaid princess who is the Ancient Weapon Poseidon. Attended the Reverie where she was nearly enslaved by Charlos before being saved by Mjosgard.

notablePeople.name: Fisher Tiger
notablePeople.role: Former Slave / Revolutionary Hero / Sun Pirates Founder
notablePeople.description: The fish-man who achieved the impossible by climbing the Red Line with his bare hands and freeing thousands of slaves 15 years ago. His raid on Mary Geoise sparked the fish-man liberation movement.

notablePeople.name: Dracule Mihawk
notablePeople.description: Attended Warlord meetings at Mary Geoise before the system's abolishment. One of the few who could walk freely in the Holy Land due to his Warlord status.

subLocations.name: Pangaea Castle
subLocations.description: The massive central castle of Mary Geoise where the Five Elders govern and where Im secretly resides. Contains the Empty Throne, the Room of Authority, and countless chambers filled with world treasures and forbidden knowledge.

subLocations.name: The Empty Throne
subLocations.description: The symbolic throne that represents equality among nations, meant to remain forever vacant. In truth, Im sits upon it in secret, making a mockery of the World Government's founding principle.

subLocations.name: Celestial Dragon Estate District
subLocations.description: The opulent living quarters of the World Nobles, filled with mansions and estates built on slave labor. Each family has elaborate compounds where they indulge in excess while slaves suffer.

subLocations.name: Reverie Assembly Hall
subLocations.description: The grand hall where world leaders gather every four years for the Reverie. Features seating for 50 kingdoms, though real decisions are made by the Five Elders in private.

subLocations.name: Slave Housing and Pens
subLocations.description: The brutal holding areas where slaves are kept, branded with the Hoof of the Soaring Dragon, and broken into submission. Fisher Tiger set fire to these areas during his legendary raid.

subLocations.name: National Treasure Vault
subLocations.description: A hidden vault within Pangaea Castle containing Im's greatest secret - a giant frozen straw hat that suggests connections to the Void Century and potentially Joy Boy.

subLocations.name: Social Square
subLocations.description: The central plaza of Mary Geoise where Celestial Dragons parade and display their wealth. Location of public executions and where Charlos attempted to enslave Shirahoshi.

subLocations.name: Marijoa Entrance Gate
subLocations.description: The heavily fortified main entrance to Mary Geoise, accessible only via the Bond Bridge elevator system. The sole 'legitimate' entry point to the Holy Land.

items.name: Empty Throne
items.description: The throne that symbolizes that no single king rules the world. Twenty swords of the founding kingdoms surround it. Im secretly sits upon it, revealing the World Government's founding lie.

items.name: Giant Frozen Straw Hat
items.description: A massive straw hat kept frozen in the National Treasure vault. Im's greatest secret, possibly connected to Joy Boy or the Void Century.

items.name: Hoof of the Soaring Dragon
items.description: The cruel brand burned into slaves owned by Celestial Dragons. Represents the ultimate symbol of their ownership over human lives. Victims include Boa Hancock and Fisher Tiger.

items.name: World Noble Bubble Helmet
items.description: The distinctive bubble helmets worn by Celestial Dragons to avoid breathing the same air as commoners. Symbol of their perceived divine status.

items.name: Ancient Weapon Control
items.description: Im demonstrated the ability to target and destroy islands from Mary Geoise, likely using Uranus or Mother Flame technology to power ancient weapons.

items.name: Reverie Invitation
items.description: Official invitations sent to the 50 member kingdoms permitting them to attend the World Summit. One of the few legitimate ways to enter Mary Geoise.

items.name: Wanted Posters (Original)
items.description: The original wanted posters and bounties issued from Mary Geoise. Im was shown cutting up posters of Luffy, Blackbeard, Shirahoshi, and Vivi.

items.name: Void Century Records (Hidden)
items.description: The true history kept hidden by the World Government. Only accessible to Im and potentially the Five Elders. What Cobra died trying to learn.

items.name: Heavenly Tribute
items.description: The massive tribute collected from all member nations to fund Mary Geoise's opulence. Poor nations starve paying this 'protection' tax.

items.name: Twenty Founding Swords
items.description: The swords of the twenty founding kingdoms placed around the Empty Throne. Nineteen are present - the Nefertari sword is missing, symbolizing Alabasta's refusal to ascend.

factions.name: Celestial Dragons (World Nobles)
factions.description: The descendants of the twenty founding kingdoms who rule from Mary Geoise. They view themselves as gods and treat all others as beneath them. Possess absolute authority and impunity.

factions.name: Five Elders (Gorosei)
factions.description: The five highest-ranking Celestial Dragons who publicly govern the world. Possess immortality and monstrous yokai transformations. Serve Im in secret.

factions.name: Revolutionary Army (Infiltrators)
factions.description: Revolutionary commanders who infiltrated Mary Geoise during the Reverie to rescue Kuma and declare war on the Celestial Dragons.

factions.name: CP0 (Cipher Pol Aigis Zero)
factions.description: The elite intelligence agency that serves the Celestial Dragons directly. The 'strongest' CP unit tasked with protecting World Nobles and eliminating threats.

factions.name: Reverie Kingdoms
factions.description: The allied nations that pay Heavenly Tribute and attend the Reverie. Most are unaware of the corruption at Mary Geoise's core.

factions.name: Holy Knights
factions.description: The mysterious elite force that serves the Celestial Dragons. Little is known about them except they are considered the 'greatest force' of the World Nobles.

factions.name: Slaves (Victims)
factions.description: The human chattel of Mary Geoise, branded and broken. Some like Fisher Tiger fought back and escaped, inspiring resistance movements.

events: 800 years ago: Twenty kingdoms form World Government, establish Mary Geoise
Nefertari Family refuses to ascend, returns to Alabasta
Im takes Empty Throne in secret
Centuries of Celestial Dragon rule and slavery
15 years ago: Fisher Tiger climbs Red Line, frees slaves, burns city
Recent Reverie begins - 50 kingdoms gather
Luffy's allies attend: Vivi, Shirahoshi, Rebecca, others
Charlos attempts to enslave Shirahoshi
Mjosgard saves Shirahoshi, opposes fellow World Nobles
Revolutionary Army commanders infiltrate Mary Geoise
Sabo and revolutionaries rescue Bartholomew Kuma
King Cobra questions Five Elders about Poneglyphs and D. Clan
Cobra meets Im in Empty Throne room
Im murders Cobra for forbidden knowledge
Sabo witnesses murder and Im's existence
Im destroys Lulusia Kingdom with ancient weapon
Cobra passes information about Nefertari D. lineage before death
Vivi goes missing after Reverie
Sabo escapes, becomes 'Flame Emperor'
Sabo falsely accused of assassinating Cobra
Mjosgard executed for opposing Celestial Dragons
World learns of Reverie chaos
Shanks meets Five Elders to discuss 'a certain pirate'
Warlord system abolished during Reverie
Im's freezer containing giant straw hat revealed
Im cuts up wanted posters of Luffy, Blackbeard, Shirahoshi, Vivi

quickFacts: Capital of the World Government for 800 years
Home of the Celestial Dragons (World Nobles)
Site of the Empty Throne (secretly occupied by Im)
Located atop the Red Line, 10,000 meters above sea level
Human slavery is legal and normalized
Fisher Tiger climbed the Red Line and freed thousands of slaves
King Cobra was murdered here by Im
Sabo witnessed Im's existence during the Reverie
Contains a giant frozen straw hat in secret vault
Reverie held every 4 years with 50 kingdoms
Striking a Celestial Dragon summons a Marine Admiral
Birthplace of the world's greatest inequality

videos.title: Im Destroys Lulusia Kingdom
videos.description: The moment Im uses an ancient weapon to completely erase Lulusia Kingdom from existence, demonstrating the terrifying power held in Mary Geoise.

videos.title: Cobra's Death and the Truth of Im
videos.description: King Cobra's tragic death after learning forbidden knowledge, and Sabo's witness of Im sitting on the Empty Throne.

videos.title: Fisher Tiger's Legendary Raid
videos.description: The flashback to Fisher Tiger's impossible climb up the Red Line and his heroic liberation of Mary Geoise's slaves.
